response.justification: The candidate is a skilled full-stack software developer with roughly 17 years of hands-on engineering experience, but the profile is fundamentally misaligned with a director-level individual contributor role focused on AI strategy. Their AI exposure is limited to a few months at their current employer (April 2025–present) working on MCP tooling, with no demonstrated history of building or advising on AI platforms at scale. The role demands recognized AI expertise, strategic advisory capability, and the ability to influence enterprise customers and partners — competencies that are absent from this resume. The candidate excels as a practitioner and craftsperson, not as a technical strategist or thought leader at the seniority level required.
strengths: 20 years of diverse engineering experience spanning web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure — demonstrates genuine technical breadth
Entrepreneurial track record: founded GISCollective (open-source geo-data platform, ongoing since 2016) and a history of freelance product delivery
Active knowledge-sharing through workshops and conference talks, indicating communication skills relevant to an advisory role
concerns: AI experience is superficial and very recent (April 2025–present); no demonstrated history of building or shipping AI/ML products or platforms
Current title and scope is Senior Software Developer — well below the director-level individual contributor seniority this role requires
No cloud architecture experience at scale, no graduate degree in an AI-related field, and no evidence of advising enterprise customers or shaping technical strategy
